rulings.Case Title: FIRST BANK & TRUST VS. BENSAL & COBURN INVESTMENTS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Vacate Renewal Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 5, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANT MICHAEL BENSAL's Motion To Vacate Renewal Of Judgment. Denied. The Application for Renewal of Judgment was timely presented to the Court along with the appropriate fee. (See Trembly Declaration.) The clerk had no legal basis upon which to reject the filing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 5, 2013, Line 12, PLAINTIFF TARGET NATIONAL BANK's Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. The claim of exemption is granted. At the hearing on June 28, 2013, judgment debtor provided documents which shown that his Bank of America checking account ending in 05330 is a deposit account within the meaning of CCP § 704.080. In addition, the Sheriff's Department has indicated that it is currently not holding any money for this account. rulings.Case Title: CAROL ALFARO VS. MICHAEL ALFARO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Leave To Amend Answer
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 5, 2013, Line 2, DEFENDANTs MICHAEL ALFARO, MACADA INC., TITAN MANAGEMENT GROUP LLC & AZTECH MAINTENANCE/DEVELOPMENT LLC's Motion For Leave To Amend Answer. The Court denies the motion because: (1) the motion is untimely; (2) granting the motion at this stage of the case would be prejudicial to plaintiff; and (3) the Federal and California schedule K-1's from 1999 through 2011 attached as Exhibit B to the Meredith Declaration in opposition to the motion show that plaintiff ("shareholder 2") and defendant ("shareholder 1") each own 50% of Macada, Inc. and belie defendant's claim of error. Defendant's reference to the letter dated November 12, 1999, is not compelling. That letter refers to "Macada," not Macada, Inc. The November 10, 1999, letter refers to Macada, LLC and Macada, Inc. and the November 12, 1999 letter could be referring to the LLC. Further, it does not address stock ownership; it refers only to "active involvement." In his Supplemental Declaration in Opposition to the motion, Defendant explicitly declines to address the schedule K-1's, and states that he "will address those at trial." (Michael Alfaro Supplemental Declaration, para. 10.) Without a further explanation regarding the tax filings the Court cannot conclude that the verified answer was the result of a mistake that is sufficient to justify the late amendment of the answer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JANNICKE KOLDERUP et al VS. OKLS, INC., DOING BUSINESS AS OKELL'S FIREPLACE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Conduct Mental Examinations On Marshall Lane And Jannicke Kolderup And To Compel Response To Written Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 5, 2013, Line 4, DEFENDANT OLKS, INC., DOING BUSINESS AS OKELL'S FIREPLACE's Motion For Leave To Conduct Mental Examinations On Marshall Lane And Jannicke Kolderup And To Compel Response To Written Discovery. Judge Miller discloses and will disclose at the hearing that she is acquainted with Dr. Sandra Klein, who was a consultant in a case Judge Miller was involved with in approximately 1991 when Judge Miller was a federal prosecutor. This will not affect Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial in this case. The motion for leave to conduct mental examinations of Marshall Lane and Jannicke Kolderup is granted, for good cause shown. The parties shall meet and confer to select dates for the mental examinations to take place. The motion in all other respects is off calendar per the parties' stipulation and letters to the Court. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MARK CATO VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF S.F. (A.P.S.)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 5, 2013, Line 5, PLAINTIFF MARK CATO's Motion For Judgment. The motion is denied. The motion appears to be seeking a default and a default judgment. Department 302 does not enter defaults and default judgments. In addition, service of the summons and complaint at on Defendant at Fox Plaza is improper under CCP § 416.50(a). rulings.Case Title: ERIC EDELSON et al VS. BOLEX LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Joinder) And Non-Oppos, Pursuant To Settlement To Bolex, Llc'S Motion To Re-Open Case For Limited Purposes Of Sealing Courr Records; Motion To Seal Court Records
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 5, 2013, Line 13, PLAINTIFFs ERIC EDELSON and ERIKA EDELSON's (Joinder) And Non-Oppos, Pursuant To Settlement To Bolex, Llc'S Motion To Re-Open Case For Limited Purposes Of Sealing Coury Records; Motion To Seal Court Records. Denied. Court records are presumed to be open unless they are required by law to be confidential. The moving party has failed to provide facts sufficient to justify sealing of the Complaint, the Cross Complaint, all responsive pleadings, the motion to expunge lis pendens and all papers filed in support and opposition, the motion for preliminary injunction and all papers filed in support and opposition, and this motion. Moving party has not established that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to these records, that this overriding interest supports sealing the record, that a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if these records are not sealed, that the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored, and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. See generally California Rules of Court 2.550(b)(1) and 2.550(d). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JULIE WALKER VS. PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel Pltfs Deposition For Sanctions And For A Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 5, 2013, Line 11, DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Deposition For Sanctions And For A Protective Order. Defendant's motion to compel plaintiff's deposition is granted. The deposition of Plaintiff shall be taken on two consecutive days in July 2013. The deposition shall take place in Sacramento, unless the parties agree otherwise. The Court encourages the parties to arrange for the production of documents requested in the notice of deposition in advance of the deposition itself, so that the deposition can proceed more efficiently. The parties must promptly meet and confer in good faith -- either in person or on the telephone -- to select the dates and exact location for the deposition. The attorney participating in the meet and confer discussion for each side must know the available dates of its client and co-counsel, and must be prepared and have authority to agree to the deposition dates and location. If the deposition cannot be completed in two consecutive days in July 2013, counsel must meet and confer in person or on the telephone to conclude the deposition at a later time. Monetary sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
